description: Suitable for Band 7.0–8.5 target. Complex visual data and abstract essay topics requiring sophisticated language and argument.

task1.title: Process Diagram — Recycling Paper
task1.description: The diagram below shows how paper is recycled. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features.
tips: Use passive voice: 'The paper is collected…', 'The pulp is bleached…'
Sequence language: first, then, following this, subsequently, finally
Cover every stage — don't skip steps
No data comparison needed — describe the process chronologically

structure.label: Introduction
structure.desc: What the diagram shows — a cyclical/linear process.

structure.label: Overview
structure.desc: How many stages, where it starts and ends.

structure.label: Body 1
structure.desc: Stages 1–4 with precise linking language.

structure.label: Body 2
structure.desc: Stages 5–8: bleaching through final product.

task1.title: Map — Town Development Over Time
task1.description: The maps below show a small town in 1970 and 2020. Summarise the main changes that occurred.
tips: Focus on change, not static description — what was added, removed, relocated?
Use location language: to the north, in the eastern part, adjacent to
Group related changes together by area
Use passive: 'The factory was demolished', 'A park was built'

structure.label: Introduction
structure.desc: Paraphrase — what the maps show and the time period.

structure.label: Overview
structure.desc: The overall character of change (development, demolition, expansion).

structure.label: Body 1
structure.desc: Changes in the northern / eastern areas with specifics.

structure.label: Body 2
structure.desc: Changes in remaining areas — what remained the same.

task1.title: Mixed Charts — Employment and GDP
task1.description: The bar chart shows employment rates in five sectors in 2000 and 2020, while the line graph shows GDP growth over the same period.
tips: Identify the relationship between the two charts — correlate trends
Describe each chart separately in Body paragraphs, but link them in the Overview
Prioritise the most significant data points
Use a sophisticated overview that synthesises both charts

structure.label: Introduction
structure.desc: What both charts show and the time period.

structure.label: Overview
structure.desc: Key trend in each chart + any relationship between them.

structure.label: Body 1
structure.desc: Employment data — sectors with biggest changes.

structure.label: Body 2
structure.desc: GDP growth trend linked to employment changes.

task2.title: Problem-Solution — Urban Traffic
task2.question: Traffic congestion in cities is a serious and growing problem. What are the causes of this problem and what measures could be taken to solve it?
tips: Clearly separate causes from solutions in different paragraphs
Each paragraph = one main idea, fully developed with evidence
Use modal verbs for solutions: could, should, might, would
Be specific — avoid vague phrases like 'things would improve'

structure.label: Introduction
structure.desc: Introduce topic + state you'll cover causes & solutions.

structure.label: Causes
structure.desc: 2 main causes of congestion, well explained.

structure.label: Solutions
structure.desc: 2 practical solutions directly linked to causes.

structure.label: Conclusion
structure.desc: Summarise key points. Optional: future outlook.

keyPhrases: One of the primary causes is …
This leads to a situation in which …
A possible solution would be to …

task2.title: Discursive Essay — Globalisation and Culture
task2.question: Globalisation has led to a growing dominance of Western culture over local traditions. To what extent do you agree or disagree with this statement?
tips: This requires nuance — a partial agreement or qualified position scores higher
Acknowledge the opposing side before rebutting it
Use abstract nouns: 'cultural homogenisation', 'indigenous heritage'
Support each point with a concrete cross-cultural example

structure.label: Introduction
structure.desc: Introduce globalisation debate + nuanced thesis.

structure.label: Body 1 (Agree)
structure.desc: Evidence that Western culture is dominant + example.

structure.label: Body 2 (Qualify)
structure.desc: Counter-evidence: resilience of local cultures.

structure.label: Conclusion
structure.desc: Balanced overall position — agree to an extent.

keyPhrases: While … , it would be an overstatement to claim …
In conclusion, although … , it is equally true that …

task2.title: Argumentative Essay — Artificial Intelligence
task2.question: Artificial intelligence will eventually replace most human jobs, leading to widespread unemployment. To what extent do you agree or disagree with this prediction?
tips: Engage with the future tense and speculation: 'may', 'could', 'is likely to'
Distinguish between types of work — manual vs creative vs managerial
A sophisticated essay acknowledges complexity, not just agreement/disagreement
Use academic hedging: 'it seems plausible that', 'evidence suggests'

structure.label: Introduction
structure.desc: Context of AI + clear but nuanced thesis.

structure.label: Body 1 (Agree)
structure.desc: Jobs AI will replace + evidence (manufacturing, data entry).

structure.label: Body 2 (Qualify)
structure.desc: Jobs resistant to automation + new roles AI creates.

structure.label: Conclusion
structure.desc: Qualified position — transformation not elimination.

keyPhrases: However, it is worth noting that …
Rather than replacing workers entirely, AI is more likely to …
In conclusion, the impact of AI will be transformative rather than simply destructive …
